data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The simultaneous collapse was a coordinated revolutionary conspiracy by disenfranchised chariot-warrior aristocracies across polities who systematically dismantled palace redistribution networks they had come to perceive as extracting surplus without providing martial protection","mechanism": "Intermarriage and gift-exchange networks among Anatolian, Aegean, and Levantine warrior elites created transnational class consciousness; palace systems dependent on chariot warfare for legitimacy collapsed when warrior aristocracies collectively withdrew cooperation and redirected loyalty to emerging ethno-linguistic identities","scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city-states"], "excludes": ["Egypt", "Mesopotamia", "Cyprus", "Sea Peoples as external invasion"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Archaeological evidence of palace destruction layers showing exclusively external military assault rather than internal sabotage or abandonment", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Contemporary treaties or correspondence demonstrating continued palace-warrior aristocracy mutual dependence and absence of class-struggle discourse", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Egyptian records showing New Kingdom chariot aristocracy similarly restive but successfully suppressed by distinct institutional mechanism rather than structurally satisfied", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Post-collapse material culture continuity demonstrating that putative revolutionary aristocracies did not in fact seize power but suffered equal collapse", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Highly atypical Marxisant class-analysis transposed to Bronze Age; deliberately provocative in treating Sea Peoples as ideological cover for internal class struggle rather than external actors."}
